heading: Special agents of the Defense Criminal Investigative Service: authority to execute warrants and make arrests
label: 1585a Special agents of the Defense Criminal Investigative Service: authority to execute warrants and make arrests

children.text: to execute and serve any warrant or other process issued under the authority of the United States; and

children.label: A
children.text: for any offense against the United States committed in the presence of that agent; and

children.label: B
children.text: for any felony cognizable under the laws of the United States if the agent has probable cause to believe that the person to be arrested has committed or is committing the felony.

children.heading: .—Agents To Have Authority
children.label: b .—Agents To Have Authority
children.text: Subsection (a) applies to any DCIS special agent whose duties include conducting, supervising, or coordinating investigations of criminal activity in programs and operations of the Department of Defense.

children.heading: .—Guidelines on Exercise of Authority
children.label: c .—Guidelines on Exercise of Authority
children.text: The authority provided under subsection (a) shall be exercised in accordance with guidelines prescribed by the Inspector General of the Department of Defense and approved by the Attorney General and any other applicable guidelines prescribed by the Secretary of Defense or the Attorney General.

children.heading: .—DCIS Special Agent Defined
children.label: d .—DCIS Special Agent Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “DCIS special agent” means an employee of the Department of Defense who is a special agent of the Defense Criminal Investigative Service (or any successor to that service).
